# dx_icons

> dx_icons provides popular open-source icon sets as native Dioxus 0.7 components for Rust. Each icon set is its own crate, with a meta-crate (`dx_icons`) that re-exports them behind feature flags.

## Installation

Add to `Cargo.toml` using the meta-crate with feature flags:

```toml
[dependencies]
dx_icons = { version = "0.1", features = ["lucide"] }
```

Available features: `feather` (default), `lucide`, `heroicons`, `tabler`, `simple`

Or depend on an icon set crate directly:

```toml
[dependencies]
dx_icons_lucide = "0.1"
```

## Icon Sets

- `dx_icons_feather` — 287 Feather Icons (stroke-based, 24×24)
- `dx_icons_lucide` — ~1700 Lucide Icons (stroke-based, 24×24, Feather successor)
- `dx_icons_heroicons` — 324 outline + 324 solid Heroicons (TWO separate enums: `HeroiconsOutline` and `HeroiconsSolid`)
- `dx_icons_tabler` — ~5000 Tabler Icons (stroke-based, 24×24)
- `dx_icons_simple` — ~3400 Simple Icons brand logos (fill-only, 24×24)

## API — Stroke-based icon sets (Feather, Lucide, Tabler)

Each crate exports: an `Icon` component, an enum (e.g. `LucideIcon`), and shorthand components (e.g. `IconHome`).

```rust
use dx_icons::lucide::*;  // or: use dx_icons_lucide::*;

rsx! {
    // Enum-based — full control over props
    Icon { icon: LucideIcon::Home }
    Icon { icon: LucideIcon::Search, size: 32, color: "red", stroke_width: 1.5 }

    // Shorthand components — same props minus `icon`
    IconHome {}
    IconSearch { size: 32, color: "#333", class: "my-icon" }
}
```

Props for `Icon` component:

| Prop           | Type     | Default          | Notes                        |
|----------------|----------|------------------|------------------------------|
| `icon`         | Enum     | required         | e.g. `LucideIcon::Home`     |
| `size`         | `u32`    | `24`             | Width and height in px       |
| `color`        | `String` | `"currentColor"` | Stroke or fill color         |
| `stroke_width` | `f32`    | `2.0`            | SVG stroke width             |
| `class`        | `String` | `""`             | Additional CSS classes       |
| `fill`         | `bool`   | `false`          | `true` = use fill, no stroke |

Shorthand components (e.g. `IconHome`, `IconSearch`) accept the same props except `icon`.

Feather uses enum `FeatherIcon`, Lucide uses `LucideIcon`, Tabler uses `TablerIcon`. The pattern is identical across all three.

## API — Heroicons (outline + solid)

Heroicons has TWO enums and TWO base components:

```rust
use dx_icons::heroicons::*;  // or: use dx_icons_heroicons::*;

rsx! {
    // Outline (stroke-based, default stroke_width: 1.5)
    IconOutline { icon: HeroiconsOutline::Home }
    IconOutline { icon: HeroiconsOutline::MagnifyingGlass, size: 32, color: "red", stroke_width: 2.0 }

    // Solid (fill-based)
    IconSolid { icon: HeroiconsSolid::Home }
    IconSolid { icon: HeroiconsSolid::MagnifyingGlass, size: 32, color: "blue" }

    // Shorthand components use Outline/Solid suffix
    IconHomeOutline {}
    IconHomeSolid { size: 32 }
}
```

`IconOutline` props: `icon` (HeroiconsOutline), `size`, `color`, `stroke_width` (default 1.5), `class`
`IconSolid` props: `icon` (HeroiconsSolid), `size`, `color`, `class`

## API — Simple Icons (brand logos)

Simple Icons are fill-only (no stroke). The `Icon` component and shorthand components have a reduced prop set:

```rust
use dx_icons::simple::*;  // or: use dx_icons_simple::*;

rsx! {
    Icon { icon: SimpleIcon::Github }
    Icon { icon: SimpleIcon::Docker, size: 32, color: "#2496ED" }

    IconGithub {}
    IconRust { size: 48, color: "#000000" }
}
```

Props: `icon` (SimpleIcon), `size` (u32, default 24), `color` (String, default "currentColor"), `class` (String).

No `stroke_width` or `fill` props — these icons are always filled.

Brand names starting with digits are prefixed with `N` in the enum, e.g. `SimpleIcon::N1password`.

## Enum naming convention

Icon names are kebab-case in the source data and converted to PascalCase for Rust enum variants:
- `arrow-right` → `ArrowRight`
- `chevron-down` → `ChevronDown`
- `magnifying-glass` → `MagnifyingGlass`

- [crates.io — dx_icons_lucide](https://crates.io/crates/dx_icons_lucide)
- [crates.io — dx_icons_heroicons](https://crates.io/crates/dx_icons_heroicons)
- [crates.io — dx_icons_tabler](https://crates.io/crates/dx_icons_tabler)
- [crates.io — dx_icons_simple](https://crates.io/crates/dx_icons_simple)
